comments: Tricky ID. Seen distantly with a scope. Prominent white wing patch. White mark under eye. Yellowish-orange bill. First impression was wing patch too broad and large for velvet scoter. Thought it was a White-winged Scoter Closer examination of photos suggests Velvet is proper ID. Poor photos. Open and eager for correction
3 Female . The fourth bird looked young and didn't fly it just paddled over the water. Photos to follow.
The bird that has been here for several days
Female type, unusually early returning bird
Spotted in series of rapids in the creek. Watched it for about 15 minutes as it swam through the water and then preened. What a cool bird and lifer!
Including 1 juvenile being fed a small man mammal
